dt-button.name: columnOrder
dt-button.summary: A button that will control the ordering on a single column
dt-button.description:
Buttons created by extending this button type will have the ability to control the ordering of a column - i.e. when activated the table will order by the target column.
The behaviour of the button will depend on the state of the table:
* If already ordering by the target column, the ordering will be inverted. * If not year ordering by the target column, the first ordering in `-init columns.orderSequence` will be used.
In both cases the order will immediately be applied and the table redrawn.
Additionally, this button type makes use of _split_ buttons to allow a tidy UI to be displayed to the end user, while still giving them full control. In this case, the sub-buttons will give the end user the ability to specify which direction of ordering should be applied. If `-init columns.orderSequence` is used to restrict the ordering to a specific direction, the split buttons will not be shown.
dt-button.option: The button's display text. By default the button title will match the title of the target column. Please note, that for the sub-buttons, if you wish to translate the `ascending` and `descending` phrases, please use `buttons.order.asc` and `buttons.order.desc`, respectively, in the DataTables `-init language` object.
The button's class name. See `b-init buttons.buttons.className` for details.
A selector for the column that will be managed by this button. You can use a column index, or a selector, as defined by the column selector option. Only a single column should be selected.
